target: data/wins.json WIN-025 — three targeted field replacements bringing WIN-025 into cluster-consistent vocabulary with WIN-010 (post-EXP-220) and WIN-068 (post-EXP-222). Replaces stale 'refraction cascade' and 'sun's physical position seasonally indeterminate' phrasing with the current 'Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse)' cross-reference and n(r) contamination framing. Concedes the FSF = H(r)/r geometric derivation (Pearson r ≈ 0.9999) that WIN-010/WIN-068 now acknowledge, while retaining the critique on absolute-magnitude observation-anchoring. Resolves ISS-1419 (EXP-222 cross-WIN consistency flag).
patch_instructions: Three edits to the WIN with id='025' in data/wins.json. Each patch is a full-string swap of an entire field (finding, detail_verdict_text, tldr_verdict) verified to equal the pre-image exactly. Other WIN-025 fields (claim, verdict, detail_claim, detail_evidence, detail_extra, tldr_evidence, detail_group, code_analysis) are untouched.

patches.before: Eclipse-induced ±10 nT magnetic variations are predicted by standard ionospheric Sq current physics (Chapman 1933). The dome provides no derived magnitude or mechanism
patches.after: Eclipse-induced ±10 nT magnetic variations are predicted by standard ionospheric Sq current physics (Chapman 1933; Kim & Chang 2018). The dome derives the station-to-station FSF ratio from its own geometry but not the absolute magnitude, which is imported from the 2017 BOU observation and reused as the 2026 baseline input
patches.rationale: Old finding said 'dome provides no derived magnitude or mechanism.' The EXP-220/EXP-222 concession — FSF = H(r)/r is a real geometric derivation — now applies at the cluster level. New finding concedes the ratio derivation while keeping the critique on the absolute-magnitude observation-anchor. Also adds Kim & Chang (2018) as the quantitative-confirmation anchor, consistent with WIN-010/WIN-068 findings.

patches.before: Eclipse-induced geomagnetic variations of 5–30 nT have been predicted by standard ionospheric Sq current physics since Chapman (1933). The dome provides no independent derivation or mechanism — it relabels a 90-year-old standard prediction as ‘aetheric.’ The dome's refraction cascade further undermines the FSF derivation claim: if the dome's own n(r) medium makes the sun's physical position seasonally indeterminate, any FSF ratio anchored to dome-coordinate geometry inherits that indeterminacy (see Section 1.8). WIN-025 shares this mechanism with WIN-010 and WIN-068 — counting the same eclipse geomagnetic observation three times inflates the WIN count without adding independent evidence.
patches.after: Eclipse-induced geomagnetic variations of 5–30 nT have been predicted by standard ionospheric Sq current physics since Chapman (1933) and quantitatively confirmed across the 39-eclipse INTERMAGNET ensemble by Kim & Chang (2018, Advances in Space Research 61:2040–2054). The dome publishes a scaling equation (delta_Z = baseline × coverage × FSF) and the FSF = H(r)/r station-to-station ratio is a genuine geometric derivation from dome parameters (Pearson r ≈ 0.9999, verified independently in the WIN-010/WIN-068 cluster) — we retract our earlier characterization of FSF as empirical. What the dome does not derive is the absolute magnitude at the Boulder anchor; that number is imported from the 2017 observation and reused as the 2026 baseline input. Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse) shows the disc-radius values FSF feeds on are themselves computed from observed Polaris elevation angles that travel through the same n(r) medium the dome concedes is 300–6,000× more refractive than Earth's atmosphere, so every dome-coordinate r in the FSF table inherits that contamination. WIN-025 shares this mechanism with WIN-010 and WIN-068 — counting the same eclipse geomagnetic observation three times inflates the WIN count without adding independent evidence.
patches.rationale: WIN-068 c9 Hole 4 recommendation: replace 'dome's refraction cascade' and 'sun's physical position seasonally indeterminate' (2026-04-12 pre-rename vocabulary) with the current 'Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse)' cross-reference and the n(r) contamination framing now used in WIN-010 c5/c6 and WIN-068 c9. Also folds in the FSF = H(r)/r geometric-derivation concession so a reader clicking from WIN-010 or WIN-068 to WIN-025 sees a consistent stance across the triple-count cluster. The triple-count-inflation closing sentence is preserved verbatim.

patches.before: This is a 93-year-old standard physics prediction that the dome relabels. No independent derivation or mechanism is provided.
patches.after: This is a 93-year-old standard physics prediction (Chapman 1933; Kim & Chang 2018) that the dome relabels. The dome's FSF = H(r)/r station-scaling is a genuine geometric derivation, but the absolute magnitude is imported from the 2017 observation and the coordinates FSF feeds on are contaminated by the n(r) medium (see Section 1.8 — The Coordinate Collapse).
patches.rationale: Old tldr said 'No independent derivation or mechanism is provided.' That is inconsistent with the FSF concession we now carry in WIN-010 and WIN-068. New tldr (a) cites both Chapman 1933 and Kim & Chang 2018 for cluster consistency, (b) concedes the FSF geometric derivation, (c) surfaces the magnitude-import-from-observation + coordinate-contamination pair that is the post-concession critique.

holes_addressed.hole: ISS-1419 / WIN-068 c9 Hole 4 (moderate) — WIN-025 pre-rename vocabulary drift
holes_addressed.description: WIN-025 detail_verdict_text used 'dome's refraction cascade' and 'sun's physical position seasonally indeterminate' — phrasing that predates the 2026-04-12 Section 1.8 rename (HNOTE-REFRACT-RESTRUCTURE-001). WIN-010/WIN-068 now reference 'Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse)' with n(r) 300–6,000× contamination framing. A reader navigating the triple-count cluster sees inconsistent vocabulary and inconsistent stance on FSF.
holes_addressed.fix: Replace the stale phrasing with the current cluster vocabulary; add the n(r) contamination framing that WIN-010 P6 and WIN-068 P2 use; concede FSF = H(r)/r geometric derivation with Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 cross-reference; keep the triple-count-inflation closing sentence unchanged.

holes_addressed.description: EXP-220 (WIN-010) and EXP-222 (WIN-068) both explicitly retracted the 'FSF is empirically fitted' framing. WIN-025 did not assert empirical fitting (its old text said 'no independent derivation or mechanism'), but a reader following the cluster links sees WIN-025 denying a derivation the siblings concede.
holes_addressed.fix: Add a short sentence acknowledging the FSF concession applies to WIN-025 as well, with a pointer to the WIN-010/WIN-068 Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 verification. Optional per WIN-068 c9 recommendation; executed here because it prevents a defender quote-mine across the three WINs.

new_evidence_added: Kim & Chang (2018) Advances in Space Research 61:2040–2054 — already used in WIN-010/WIN-068; now also cited in WIN-025 for cluster consistency.
Pearson r ≈ 0.9999 FSF verification — cross-referenced into WIN-025 via the WIN-010/WIN-068 cluster, no independent recomputation claimed here.
n(r) 300–6,000× contamination framing — imported verbatim from WIN-010 P6 / WIN-068 P2 to keep cluster vocabulary consistent.

anticipated_objections.objection: The old WIN-025 text was shorter and punchier. The new text is three sentences longer — isn't that dilution?
anticipated_objections.response: The new text is ~480 words; the old was ~150 words. The length increase is load-bearing: the old text carried two stale-vocabulary phrases that were factually misleading after the EXP-220/EXP-222 concessions. Terseness that misleads is worse than accuracy that runs long. The tldr_verdict remains short (2 sentences) for the skim reader.

anticipated_objections.objection: Isn't it bad to concede the FSF derivation in WIN-025 when WIN-025's own text never made the empirical-fitting claim?
anticipated_objections.response: We are not retracting anything WIN-025 said; we are adding a one-clause acknowledgment to keep the cluster readable. The alternative — WIN-025 silent on FSF while WIN-010/WIN-068 concede it — tempts a defender to claim we move our concession between tabs. Explicit, consistent acknowledgment closes that move. WIN-068 c9 Hole 4 explicitly calls this 'optional' but notes it helps the cluster; the cost is one sentence.

anticipated_objections.objection: The new P1 brings in Kim & Chang (2018) even though the old text only cited Chapman 1933. Is that a new citation requiring independent verification?
anticipated_objections.response: Kim & Chang (2018) is already cited and verified in WIN-010 (EXP-220) and WIN-068 (EXP-222) texts. WIN-068 c9 citation_check_unchecked list notes the publisher-page verification is still open across the cluster, but the paper's identity (author, title, journal, volume, page range) has been stable across three cycles. We are importing a citation that is already in cluster use, not minting a new one.

anticipated_objections.objection: The closing triple-count sentence references 'WIN-068' — should we also say 'the 39-eclipse ensemble' to match the new P1?
anticipated_objections.response: The closing sentence targets the triple-count inflation argument, which is about how many separate WINs the cluster buys with one mechanism. Identifying the three WINs by ID is the clearest form. Re-describing WIN-068 as 'the 39-eclipse ensemble' in the same sentence would double-identify and add no information. The preserved-verbatim closing is intentional.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author is entitled to a consistent reading of our critique across the WIN-010/WIN-025/WIN-068 cluster. WIN-025 sat uncorrected while its siblings were rewritten, giving the dome a free defender-move ('which version of your critique are we responding to?'). This EXP closes the vocabulary and concession gap without changing the verdict (Std Model Explains) and without adding new arguments not already in the cluster. The vocabulary update is overdue maintenance, not a substantive concession.
cross_win_consistency_flag: After integration, WIN-010, WIN-025, and WIN-068 will all use 'Section 1.8 (The Coordinate Collapse)' cross-reference and the n(r) 300–6,000× contamination framing. Finding fields across the three WINs will all concede the FSF station-scaling derivation and critique only the absolute-magnitude observation-anchor. WIN-068 finding is blocked on its own parallel EXP per WIN-068 c9 Hole 1 (critical) — that work is out of scope here but should be prioritized.
out_of_scope_deliberately: WIN-068 finding field retracted-framing fix (WIN-068 c9 Hole 1 critical) — separate EXP required.
WIN-068 detail_evidence paragraph 3 'by contrast' rhetorical fix (c9 Hole 3 moderate) — separate EXP.
WIN-068 detail_evidence paragraph 2 'refraction cascade-era' aside harmonization (c9 Hole 8 minor) — can be bundled with a future cluster polish EXP.
WIN-010 c6 Holes 3/4/5/6/7/8 — all handled separately (some in companion EXP-228, others flagged for follow-up).
No change to WIN-025 detail_evidence, detail_claim, detail_extra, tldr_evidence, claim, verdict, detail_group, code_analysis.
propagation_note: EXP-228 (companion this run) tightens WIN-010 finding + P1 + P7 along the same cluster-consistent lines. EXP-229 propagates the same concession-plus-critique stance into WIN-025. WIN-068 parallel finding-field fix is still outstanding — should be the next queue push.
